title: 🦞 Discord - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Discord. Ready for DMs and guild channels via the official Discord gateway. Discord DMs default to pairing mode. You will need to create a new application with a bot, add the bot to your server, and pair it to OpenClaw. ### Create a Discord application and bot. Set the **Username** to whatever you call your OpenClaw agent. This is your **Bot Token** and you will need it shortly. You'll generate an invite URL with the right permissions to add the bot to your server. ### Enable Developer Mode and collect your IDs. Back in the Discord app, you need to enable Developer Mode so you can copy internal IDs. Save your **Server ID** and **User ID** alongside your Bot Token — you'll send all three to OpenClaw in the next step. For pairing to work, Discord needs to allow your bot to DM you. Use `channels.discord.applicationId` for the default account, or `channels.discord.accounts.<accountId>.applicationId` when you run multiple Discord bots.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw x Discord Server Integration Guide | MI
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw x Discord Server Integration Guide. Architecture diagram of OpenClaw integrated with Discord server. After embedding an OpenClaw AI agent into a Discord server, your community gains the following capabilities:. Compared to Telegram's personal remote control scenario or Slack's enterprise workflow scenario, the Discord x OpenClaw integration is best suited for **community management, open-source project support, and large-scale multi-user interaction**. Step 1: Create a Discord Bot and Obtain Token. This is the foundation of the entire **OpenClaw Discord setup** process -- creating an Application and Bot on the Discord Developer Portal and obtaining the necessary authentication Token. ### 3.1 Create a Discord Application. ### 3.2 Create Bot and Obtain Token. **Important security reminder:** The Bot Token is equivalent to full access credentials for the Bot -- anyone who has it can control your Bot. Never expose the Token in public places (GitHub, Discord channels, screenshots). After obtaining the Discord Bot Token, the next step is to write it into OpenClaw's configuration so the Gateway can connect to Discord as your Bot.

title: 🦞 Telegram - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Telegram. Long polling is the default mode; webhook mode is optional. Default DM policy for Telegram is pairing. ### Create the bot token in BotFather. `{channels: {telegram: { enabled: true, botToken: "123:abc", dmPolicy: "pairing", groups: { "*": { requireMention: true } },},},}`. Telegram does **not** use `openclaw channels login telegram`; configure token in config/env, then start gateway. ### Add the bot to a group. Add the bot to your group, then get both IDs that group access needs:. For first-time setup, get the group chat ID from `openclaw logs --follow`, a forwarded-ID bot, or Bot API `getUpdates`. After the group is allowed, `/whoami@<bot_username>` can confirm the user and group IDs. Negative Telegram supergroup IDs that start with `-100` are group chat IDs. Put them under `channels.telegram.groups`, not under `groupAllowFrom`. Practical pattern for one-owner bots: set your user ID in `channels.telegram.allowFrom`, leave `groupAllowFrom` unset, and allow the target groups under `channels.telegram.groups`.

title: 🦞 Scheduled tasks · OpenClaw
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# List all jobsopenclaw cron list # Get one stored job as JSONopenclaw cron get <jobId> # Show one job, including resolved delivery routeopenclaw cron show <jobId> # Edit a jobopenclaw cron edit <jobId> --message "Updated prompt" --model "opus" # Force run a job nowopenclaw cron run <jobId> # Force run a job now and wait for its terminal statusopenclaw cron run <jobId> --wait --wait-timeout 10m --poll-interval 2s # Run only if dueopenclaw cron run <jobId> --due # View run historyopenclaw cron runs --id <jobId> --limit 50 # View one exact runopenclaw cron runs --id <jobId> --run-id <runId> # Delete a jobopenclaw cron remove <jobId> # Agent selection (multi-agent setups)openclaw cron create "0 6 * * *" "Check ops queue" --name "Ops sweep" --session isolated --agent opsopenclaw cron edit <jobId> --clear-agent. [Quick start](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#quick-start)[How cron works](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#how-cron-works)[Schedule types](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#schedule-types)[Day-of-month and day-of-week use OR logic](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#day-of-month-and-day-of-week-use-or-logic)[Execution styles](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#execution-styles)[Payload options for isolated jobs](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#payload-options-for-isolated-jobs)[Delivery and output](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#delivery-and-output)[Output language](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#output-language)[CLI examples](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#cli-examples)[Webhooks](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#webhooks)[Authentication](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#authentication)[Gmail PubSub integration](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#gmail-pubsub-integration)[Wizard setup (recommended)](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#wizard-setup-(recommended))[Gateway auto-start](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#gateway-auto-start)[Manual one-time setup](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#manual-one-time-setup)[Gmail model override](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#gmail-model-override)[Managing jobs](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#managing-jobs)[Configuration](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#configuration)[Troubleshooting](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#troubleshooting)[Command ladder](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#command-ladder)[Related](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs#related).

title: 🦞 Cron - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Cron. # `openclaw cron`. Run `openclaw cron --help` for the full command surface. `openclaw cron create` is an alias for `openclaw cron add`. Use `--webhook <url>` when the job should POST the finished payload instead of delivering to a chat target:. `openclaw cron list` and `openclaw cron show <job-id>` preview the resolved delivery route. Isolated `cron add` jobs default to `--announce` delivery. Isolated cron chat delivery is shared between the agent and the runner:. Use `cron add|create --webhook <url>` or `cron edit <job-id> --webhook <url>` to set webhook delivery. `cron edit <job-id> --webhook <url>`. `openclaw cron run <job-id>` force-runs by default and returns as soon as the manual run is queued. `openclaw cron run <job-id>`. `openclaw cron run <job-id>openclaw cron runs --id <job-id> --run-id <run-id>`. `openclaw cron listopenclaw cron list --agent opsopenclaw cron get <job-id>openclaw cron show <job-id>openclaw cron run <job-id>openclaw cron run <job-id> --dueopenclaw cron run <job-id> --wait --wait-timeout 10mopenclaw cron run <job-id> --wait --wait-timeout 10m --poll-interval 2sopenclaw cron runs --id <job-id> --limit 50openclaw cron runs --id <job-id> --run-id <run-id>`.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Tip #2: Set Up Cron Jobs for Automated Daily Tasks - AiX Society 人工智能應用研究學會
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

* OpenClaw Tip #2: Set Up Cron Jobs for Automated Daily Tasks. Instead of manually asking your agent to perform repetitive tasks, you can schedule them to run automatically — morning briefings, inbox checks, data monitoring, and more. ## How OpenClaw Cron Jobs Work. Cron jobs run inside the Gateway process, not the AI model itself. openclaw cron add \ --name "Reminder" \ --at "2026-03-08T09:00:00Z" \ --session main \ --system-event "Reminder: review the weekly report" \ --wake now \ --delete-after-run. openclaw cron add \ --name "Morning brief" \ --cron "0 7 * * *" \ --tz "Asia/Hong_Kong" \ --session isolated \ --message "Summarize overnight emails, calendar for today, and top 3 AI news stories." \ --announce \ --channel telegram. # List all jobs openclaw cron list # Run a job manually openclaw cron run  # View past runs openclaw cron runs --id  # Delete a job openclaw cron delete. * AI tips, automation, cron jobs, OpenClaw, productivity.
